entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain
stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
The mantis shrimp has 16 color-receptive cones. Humans have 3. It sees ultraviolet infrared and polarized light. Its punch is faster than a bullet and can break aquarium glass.
Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Octopus Has Three Hearts
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Honeybees Recognize Faces
stories: Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
The pistol shrimp snaps its claw creating a cavitation bubble that reaches 4700°C hotter than the sun's surface. The bubble collapses louder than a gunshot stunning prey.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Immortal Jellyfish
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
The Greenland shark lives up to 500 years the longest-living vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until age 150. One born in the 1500s could still be swimming today.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain
stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Dolphins Have Names
stories: Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Penguins Propose With Pebbles

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Honeybees Recognize Faces
stories: Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Crows Hold Grudges
stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain
stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Elephants Hear With Feet

Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.

Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.
The Greenland shark lives up to 500 years the longest-living vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until age 150. One born in the 1500s could still be swimming today.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Hummingbirds Fly Backward

Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
The Greenland shark lives up to 500 years the longest-living vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until age 150. One born in the 1500s could still be swimming today.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Crows Hold Grudges
stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Octopus Has Three Hearts

entries.script: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain. The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration. Mantis Shrimp Has Super Vision. The mantis shrimp has 16 color-receptive cones. Humans have 3. It sees ultraviolet infrared and polarized light. Its punch is faster than a bullet and can break aquarium glass. Sea Otters Hold Hands. Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Pistol Shrimp Hotter Than Sun
stories: The pistol shrimp snaps its claw creating a cavitation bubble that reaches 4700°C hotter than the sun's surface. The bubble collapses louder than a gunshot stunning prey.
The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Crows Hold Grudges

stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Sloths Poop Weekly
stories: Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Elephants Hear With Feet
entries.hook: You won't believe what this animal can do:
stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
The mantis shrimp has 16 color-receptive cones. Humans have 3. It sees ultraviolet infrared and polarized light. Its punch is faster than a bullet and can break aquarium glass.
Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Elephants Hear With Feet
stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
The pistol shrimp snaps its claw creating a cavitation bubble that reaches 4700°C hotter than the sun's surface. The bubble collapses louder than a gunshot stunning prey.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Elephants Hear With Feet
stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Dolphins Have Names
stories: Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Mantis Shrimp Has Super Vision
entries.hook: You won't believe what this animal can do:
stories: The mantis shrimp has 16 color-receptive cones. Humans have 3. It sees ultraviolet infrared and polarized light. Its punch is faster than a bullet and can break aquarium glass.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Tardigrades Survive Space
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Crows Hold Grudges
stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.

stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Honeybees Recognize Faces
stories: Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
The pistol shrimp snaps its claw creating a cavitation bubble that reaches 4700°C hotter than the sun's surface. The bubble collapses louder than a gunshot stunning prey.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Mantis Shrimp Has Super Vision
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: The mantis shrimp has 16 color-receptive cones. Humans have 3. It sees ultraviolet infrared and polarized light. Its punch is faster than a bullet and can break aquarium glass.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
Rats produce ultrasonic vocalizations when tickled essentially laughter. Humans need special equipment to hear it. They seek out being tickled and return to researchers who tickle them.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.

stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Elephants Hear With Feet
stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Butterflies Taste With Feet
stories: Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.
Rats produce ultrasonic vocalizations when tickled essentially laughter. Humans need special equipment to hear it. They seek out being tickled and return to researchers who tickle them.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Octopus Has Three Hearts
entries.hook: You won't believe what this animal can do:
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Tardigrades Survive Space
entries.hook: You won't believe what this animal can do:
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
Rats produce ultrasonic vocalizations when tickled essentially laughter. Humans need special equipment to hear it. They seek out being tickled and return to researchers who tickle them.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Cows Have Best Friends
stories: Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.

Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Cows Have Best Friends
stories: Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Elephants Hear With Feet
stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Sea Otters Hold Hands
stories: Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.
The Greenland shark lives up to 500 years the longest-living vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until age 150. One born in the 1500s could still be swimming today.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
The mantis shrimp has 16 color-receptive cones. Humans have 3. It sees ultraviolet infrared and polarized light. Its punch is faster than a bullet and can break aquarium glass.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Dolphins Have Names
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
The pistol shrimp snaps its claw creating a cavitation bubble that reaches 4700°C hotter than the sun's surface. The bubble collapses louder than a gunshot stunning prey.
The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Immortal Jellyfish
stories: The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Crows Hold Grudges
stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Crows Hold Grudges
stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Dolphins Have Names
stories: Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.

Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.

Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal. Penguins Propose With Pebbles. Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Cows Have Best Friends
stories: Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
The Greenland shark lives up to 500 years the longest-living vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until age 150. One born in the 1500s could still be swimming today.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain
entries.hook: You won't believe what this animal can do:
stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
Rats produce ultrasonic vocalizations when tickled essentially laughter. Humans need special equipment to hear it. They seek out being tickled and return to researchers who tickle them.
The Greenland shark lives up to 500 years the longest-living vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until age 150. One born in the 1500s could still be swimming today.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Tardigrades Survive Space
stories: Tardigrades or water bears survive in outer space. They withstand -272°C to 150°C radiation dehydration and vacuum. They enter cryptobiosis and revive decades later.
The pistol shrimp snaps its claw creating a cavitation bubble that reaches 4700°C hotter than the sun's surface. The bubble collapses louder than a gunshot stunning prey.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.

Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration. Butterflies Taste With Feet. Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Tardigrades Survive Space

Honeybees recognize human faces using configural processing the same way humans do. They count to four understand zero and communicate flower locations through the waggle dance.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Crows Hold Grudges
stories: Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.
Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is biologically immortal. When injured or stressed it reverts to its juvenile polyp stage and grows again. It can repeat this cycle forever.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Octopus Has Three Hearts

Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. They also have the thickest fur of any mammal up to 1 million hairs per square inch because they have no blubber.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Dolphins Have Names
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: Dolphins give each other unique signature whistles within their first year. They call each other by name and remember whistles of dolphins not seen for 20 years.
Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
Cheetahs are the only big cats that cannot roar. They chirp like birds purr meow and make high-pitched sounds to communicate with cubs. They are closer to small cats than big ones.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Axolotl Can Regrow Its Brain
stories: The axolotl can regenerate entire limbs parts of its brain and spinal cord. It can regrow the same limb 5 times perfectly. Scientists study them for human tissue regeneration.
Crows remember human faces for years and warn other crows. They teach their children which humans to avoid. They use tools solve puzzles and hold funerals for their dead.

entries.title: Mother Nature has incredible secrets: Cows Have Best Friends
stories: Cows form close friendships and get stressed when separated from their best friend. Their heart rate increases and cortisol rises. Cows moo in distinct regional accents.
Sloths only defecate once a week. They climb down from trees specifically to do it their most vulnerable moment. Losing 30% of body weight in one bowel movement is normal.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Octopus Has Three Hearts

Hummingbirds are the only birds that fly backward forward and hover. They beat wings up to 80 times per second. Their heart rate reaches 1260 bpm and they eat every 10-15 minutes.

entries.title: You won't believe what this animal can do: Octopus Has Three Hearts
entries.hook: You won't believe what this animal can do:
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.

Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.

entries.title: This animal fact sounds fake but is true: Elephants Hear With Feet
entries.hook: This animal fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.
Male penguins search for the smoothest pebble and present it to a female as a proposal. If she accepts she places it in their nest. Some travel hundreds of meters for the perfect pebble.

entries.title: Nature is absolutely mind-blowing: Koala Fingerprints
stories: Koalas have fingerprints nearly identical to humans. Even under microscopes experts struggle to distinguish them. They are the only non-primate animals with true fingerprints.

entries.title: Evolution created something amazing: Octopus Has Three Hearts
stories: An octopus has three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills and one pumps to the body. When swimming the body heart stops beating. That's why octopuses prefer crawling.
Elephants detect seismic vibrations through special sensory cells in their feet. They pick up low-frequency rumbles from 20 miles away and communicate using infrasound across vast distances.
Butterflies have taste sensors on their feet. They instantly taste leaves or flowers when landing to check if suitable for laying eggs. They detect sugar as low as 0.01%.
